Table DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE

Card of table DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE

Name

DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE

Code

DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE

DBMS

Snowflake


Description of the table DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE

---------------------- Pseudocode ----------------------------------

 

 

 

1. Initialize a new table in the Presentation Layer in "DB_CONS_PL_(env)" under "PL_CONSUMERORDER" schema

called "DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE".

 

2. Define the Columns, based on the script provided.

Consider the data type for Implementation -

Text as varchar(16777216)

Integer as Number(38,0)

Date&Time as Timestamp

 

3. Define the Constraints

Primary Key (Unique Record Identifier):

DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE_KEY = MD5(Concatenation of(ID, MARKET_CODE))

 

 

 

4. Save the table definition.

 

5. Maintain Data Dictionary

i. Add the column description to each column (from Comment tab in PD).

ii. Mention the Data Classification & PII category for each column and mask them as per standard guideline.

 

6. The result in the target object MUST stay deduplicated.

 

7. Verify whether the newcoming ingested records in the source already exist in the Presentation Layer with any previous load, based on the

Unique Record Identifier.

i. If Yes, don't propagate them to the Presentation Layer.

ii. If No, propagate them to the Presentation Layer.

 

 

8. Follow the Entity & Attribute level Business Rules defined.

 

9. Run the RLS specific script based on the MARKET_CODE (applicable for QAS & Prod).

 

 

 

 

 

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-------------------------------------- Load Type -----------------------------------------------------------------

APPEND

 

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

 

 


Check constraint name of the table DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE

CKT_DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE


Server validation rule of the table DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE

%RULES%


Code preview of the table DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE

drop table DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE cascade;

--==============================================================
-- Table: DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE
--==============================================================
create or replace table PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE (
DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE_KEY TEXT not null,
ID INTEGER not null,
ORDER_ID TEXT,
GIFT_CERT_CODE TEXT,
GIFT_CERT_ID INTEGER,
AMOUNT INTEGER,
MARKET_CODE TEXT not null,
DCS_ORDER_KEY TEXT not null,
constraint PK_DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE primary key (DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE_KEY)
not enforced
);

comment on column PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE.DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE_KEY is
'Primary Key. Unique record identifier. ';

comment on column PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE.ID is
'Device identifier (guid) ';

comment on column PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE.ORDER_ID is
'Order Identifier';

comment on column PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE.AMOUNT is
'Quantity of the items';

comment on column PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE.MARKET_CODE is
'Identifier for the Market -> 2 letter Country Code + ''04''';

comment on column PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE.DCS_ORDER_KEY is
'Primary Key. Unique record identifier.';

alter table PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE
add constraint FK_DCS_GIFT_R16_ORDER_DCS_ORDE foreign key (MARKET_CODE)
references PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_ORDER (DCS_ORDER_KEY)
not enforced not deferrable;

alter table PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE
add constraint FK_DCS_GIFT_R17_ORDER_DCS_ORDE foreign key (DCS_ORDER_KEY)
references PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_ORDER (DCS_ORDER_KEY)
not enforced not deferrable;

alter table PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_GIFTCERTIFICATE
add constraint FK_DCS_GIFT_R4_GIFTCE_DCS_ORDE foreign key (ORDER_ID)
references PL_CONSUMERORDER.DCS_ORDER (DCS_ORDER_KEY)
not enforced not deferrable;
